mood.definition: the way you feel at a particular time, such as happy or sad
mood.example: Her mood changed quickly from happy to angry.

capacity.definition: the ability to do or hold something
capacity.example: Children have a great capacity for happiness when they feel loved.

invested.definition: having put a lot of time, energy, or feeling into something
invested.example: Parents have too much invested in the idea of being perfect.

gambolling.definition: running and jumping around happily, like a playful animal
gambolling.example: The children were gambolling in the field on a sunny day.

meadow.definition: a field of grass, often with wild flowers
meadow.example: They had a picnic in the meadow.

picnic.definition: a meal eaten outside, usually in a park or field
picnic.example: The family enjoyed a picnic by the river.

emotional.definition: related to feelings, or showing strong feelings
emotional.example: He had many emotional reactions to the news.

compare.definition: to look at two things to see how they are different or the same
compare.example: You need other feelings to compare happiness to.

scolded.definition: spoken to angrily because you did something wrong
scolded.example: The child was scolded for breaking the glass.

distracted.definition: having your attention taken away from what you were doing or feeling
distracted.example: She was distracted from her sadness by a funny video.

aspects.definition: different parts or sides of something
aspects.example: Parents should accept all aspects of their child's personality.

experience.definition: something that happens to you, or the knowledge you gain from living
experience.example: Every child's experience of the world is different.

overheard.definition: heard something by accident, not on purpose
overheard.example: My father overheard what I said to his friend.

remonstrate.definition: to argue against someone, to tell them they are wrong
remonstrate.example: He turned angrily to remonstrate with me about what I said.

nonsense.definition: something that makes no sense or is not true
nonsense.example: He said my feelings were nonsense.

idyllic.example: He described my childhood as idyllic, but I did not feel that way.

rubbish.definition: something that is not true or has no value (British English)
rubbish.example: He said what I felt was rubbish.

beloved.example: He was my beloved father, even though he scared me.

confused.definition: not able to think clearly, not sure what is happening
confused.example: She felt confused about her own feelings.

mistaken.definition: wrong about something, having an incorrect idea
mistaken.example: I felt I must have been mistaken about my feelings.

contradicted.definition: said the opposite of what someone else said
contradicted.example: When my father contradicted me, I felt confused.

fug.definition: a feeling of being unclear and unable to think, like being in a fog
fug.example: My brain went into an unclear fug when he told me I was wrong.

shame.definition: a painful feeling that you have done something wrong or are not good enough
shame.example: In the fug there was also shame.

viewpoint.definition: the way you see or think about something
viewpoint.example: My viewpoint was different from my father's.

valid.definition: reasonable and acceptable, having a real basis
valid.example: Her viewpoint was a valid way of seeing things.

negatives.example: He treated her sadness as a negative to be corrected.

corrected.definition: fixed or changed to be right
corrected.example: Feelings should not be treated as mistakes to be corrected.

deepen.definition: to make something stronger or more meaningful
deepen.example: Accepting your child's feelings will deepen your bond.

bond.definition: a strong feeling of closeness between people
bond.example: A parent and child share a special bond.

likelihood.definition: the chance that something will happen
likelihood.example: There is every likelihood you will increase their happiness.

foul.example: I had a foul day at work today.

batted.definition: pushed away quickly and without care
batted.example: She felt batted away by his cold response.

habitually.definition: done regularly as a habit, all the time
habitually.example: If this is the response you habitually get, you might stop sharing.

confiding.definition: sharing private or personal things with someone you trust
confiding.example: She gave up confiding in her partner about her problems.

unfair.definition: not right or equal, treating someone badly
unfair.example: She said her boss had been very unfair to her.

carelessness.definition: not paying enough attention, making mistakes because you are not careful
carelessness.example: His carelessness caused everyone extra work.

squirrel.definition: a small animal with a big tail that climbs trees
squirrel.example: Look at that cute squirrel out of the window.

sympathetic.definition: showing that you understand and care about someone's problems
sympathetic.example: It is easy to be sympathetic when your child has a bad day.

sibling.example: You cannot tell a child that they love their sibling.

container.definition: something that holds things inside it; here it means a safe space for feelings
container.example: Children need a safe container for their feelings.

revelled.example: She revelled in being told she was a big sister.

novelty.definition: the quality of being new and exciting
novelty.example: The novelty of having a new baby wore off quickly.

tantrums.definition: sudden bursts of anger, usually by a child
tantrums.example: She began having more tantrums after the baby arrived.

cooperate.definition: to work together, to do what is asked of you
cooperate.example: The child refused to cooperate at bedtime.

wetting.definition: making wet with urine, losing control of your bladder
wetting.example: She started wetting the bed again when the baby came.

exhausting.example: Caring for two small children is exhausting.

frank.definition: honest and direct in what you say
frank.example: To be frank, the bedtime was perfectly horrible.

horrible.example: It was a perfectly horrible evening.

irritated.example: I can still be irritated by my sister for no reason.

articulate.definition: to express a thought or feeling clearly in words
articulate.example: I wanted to understand her feelings and articulate them to her.

inconvenient.definition: causing problems or difficulty, not easy to deal with
inconvenient.example: Her feelings were inconvenient but still real.

inconvenienced.definition: caused trouble or difficulty for someone
inconvenienced.example: Whenever she was inconvenienced, I described how she must feel.

cooperation.definition: working together and helping each other
cooperation.example: It was great to get her cooperation back.

fetching.definition: going to get something and bringing it back
fetching.example: She started helping by fetching nappies for the baby.

nappies.definition: soft cloth or paper that babies wear to catch waste (British English for diapers)
nappies.example: She helped by fetching nappies for the baby.

wipes.definition: wet cloths used to clean a baby's skin
wipes.example: She helped by passing wipes during diaper changes.

nap.definition: a short sleep during the day
nap.example: The baby just woke up from his nap.

fountains.definition: structures where water sprays up into the air, often in parks
fountains.example: The children loved playing under the fountains in the park.

soaking.definition: very wet, completely covered in water
soaking.example: He would be soaking wet after playing in the fountains.

persuade.definition: to try to make someone believe or do something by giving reasons
persuade.example: My mum tried to persuade him he did not want to get wet.

disappointed.definition: sad because something did not happen as you hoped
disappointed.example: I'm sorry you're disappointed that we have to leave.

squabbles.definition: small, not serious fights or arguments
squabbles.example: There are squabbles between the siblings sometimes.

animosity.definition: a strong feeling of dislike or anger toward someone
animosity.example: They play together without animosity most of the time.

empathy.definition: the ability to understand and share another person's feelings
empathy.example: Empathy is harder work than it may seem.

circumstances.definition: the facts or conditions around a situation
circumstances.example: Think about their circumstances before judging them.

conclusion.definition: a decision or opinion you reach after thinking about something
conclusion.example: They came to a different conclusion about the problem.

practising.definition: doing something again and again to get better at it
practising.example: Practising empathy will make it easier over time.

validate.definition: to show that someone's feelings are real and important
validate.example: I need to validate how they are feeling.

teatime.definition: the time in the late afternoon when you have a light meal (British English)
teatime.example: By teatime her behaviour had improved.

granted.definition: accepted as true without thinking about it carefully
granted.example: Parents take it for granted that their happiness equals their child's.

failure.definition: the feeling of not succeeding or not being good enough
failure.example: You may feel like a failure if your child seems unhappy.

opportunity.definition: a chance to do something good
opportunity.example: What my father missed was an opportunity to connect with me.

scared.example: He was my beloved, if scared, father.
